info.name: Processor API
info.description:
## Card Transaction Processor

### Environments (import both from `server/postman/`)
1. **Processor API — Local** — use for day-to-day: sets `baseUrl`, `seedPassword`, and local DB hints. **Select this in the environment dropdown** before sending requests.
2. **Processor API — Production (sample)** — same *shape* as local; `baseUrl` is a placeholder. Replace with your real host before any real run. `seedPassword` is still the dev **sample** only (not a real secret).

`token*` and `lastTransactionId` are **collection** variables (filled by test scripts or by you), not the env files.

### App setup
1. Run PostgreSQL and `server/scripts/db_setup.sh` (or `db_setup.ps1`). Set **`seedPassword`** to the **plaintext** you used in Java to generate the BCrypt hashes in `09_seed_admin_user.sql` (must be 12–128 chars per API validation).
2. **Seeded users** (one password, different roles for RBAC tests):
   - **`admin`** — SUPER_ADMIN
   - **`org_admin`** — ADMIN
   - **`operator`** — OPERATOR
   - **`analyst`** — ANALYST
   - **`viewer`** — VIEWER
3. **Collection Runner order**: run folder **1. Auth** first (or the whole collection top-to-bottom) to populate `token*`.
4. **Batch upload (folder 3)**: set form field **`file`** to `server/postman/sample-batch.csv`.

### RBAC (negative tests in this collection)
- **ANALYST** — no `TRANSACTIONS_WRITE` / `BATCHES_WRITE` → 403 on create transaction and batch.
- **VIEWER** — no `TRANSACTIONS_WRITE` / `BATCHES_WRITE`; no `AUDIT_LOGS_READ` → 403 on list activity-logs, but can POST activity.
- **OPERATOR** — has batch + txn write; **no** `AUDIT_LOGS_READ` → 403 on **GET** `/activity-logs` (can POST activity).

item.name: 0. Public (no auth)
item.description: Static HTML at `/` and `/help` — no JWT.

item.name: 1. Auth
item.description: Login, `/me`, optional logout. **`{{seedPassword}}`** = plaintext matching `09_seed_admin_user.sql` hashes.

request.description: All perms except USERS_DELETE (no user APIs here).
Read txns; no manual create; has audit list.
Read-only txns/reports; no write txn or batch; no audit list.
Expect `roleCodes` to include `SUPER_ADMIN` and a non-empty `permissionCodes` array.
Revokes session for this JWT. Re-run **Login — admin (again)** before protected calls if you need a fresh token after other tests.

exec: pm.test("204 No Content or 200", function() { pm.expect([200, 204]).to.include(pm.response.code); });

request.description: Re-login after **logout** so `tokenAdmin` is valid for folders 2–5.

item.name: 2. Transactions
item.description: Manual create (POST body = JSON **array** of txn objects), get by id, list. **Requires `lastTransactionId` from the create request.**

request.description: Requires `TRANSACTIONS_WRITE`. Body must be a **non-empty JSON array** of transaction objects. Saves `lastTransactionId` from the first created row (for GET-by-id next).
Creates two accepted transactions in one request; rolls back entirely if any row fails validation or processing.
Body must be a non-empty JSON array.
Optional filters: from, to, cardBrands, minAmount, maxAmount.

item.name: 3. Batch (multipart)
item.description: **Attach `postman/sample-batch.csv`** in each `file` field. Import path is relative to repo root.

request.description: **Body → form-data:** attach `file` and text field `format`=`csv`. Requires `BATCHES_WRITE`.

item.name: 4. Reports
item.description: Optional query params: `from`, `to` (ISO-8601 instants). `daily-volume` defaults to the **last 90 days** when both are omitted; other report endpoints use a shorter default.

request.description: Default window: last **90 days** through now when `from` / `to` are omitted.
Explicit ISO-8601 `from` and `to` (half-open interval [from, to) in the service).

item.name: 5. Activity logs
item.description: List requires `AUDIT_LOGS_READ` (e.g. admin, analyst). **Operator** and **viewer** get **403** on **GET** but may **POST** (WRITE).

request.description: OPERATOR lacks `AUDIT_LOGS_READ` (only WRITE for client events).
